summary: A defensively excellent side (0 goals conceded in their qualifying mid-block reference) that builds in a back-3 and attacks with numbers, but is open to transitions.
style: 4-3-3 / 4-1-4-1 / 4-2-3-1 variations — build in a back-3 (dropping a CM), push fullbacks high & wide, wingers and 10s close to the CF, crosses and numbers into the box; drop into a mid-block.

counterReliance.note: Mid-block first, attacks with numbers; open to transition

strengths: Attack the box with numbers and crosses
weaknesses: Can be opened up at times even without conceding

keyPlayers.name: Hannibal Mejbri
keyPlayers.note: Creative dribbler in midfield — 19 chances created in a 'poor' Burnley side, 5 goal contributions

tactics: Build via a back-3, get fullbacks high, and load the box with crosses and runners, before dropping into a stingy mid-block. The concern is how easily they can be opened in transition.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
